name: Pizzettas (r T)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Cup Polenta or Coarsely Ground Cornmeal
1 Tablespoon Finely Chopped Rosemary or 1 Teaspoon Dried Rosemary
recipeInstructions:
To Make The Dough:
1. In a glass measuring cup with a lip, combine the water and sugar and stir to mix.
2. Sprinkle yeast over the top and allow to stand for 10 minutes until it has a frothy head. If no froth forms, discard yeast and start again.
3. In the bowl of a food processor fitted with the metal blade, combine all-purpose flour with polenta, whole wheat flour, rosemary, olive oil, and salt. Process briefly just to blend.
4. With motor running, pour yeast mixture steadily through feed tube and process until a rough ball forms on the central stem. If dough does not form within 20 seconds, remove cover, sprinkle additional water over dough, then process again. Total processing time should be about 45 seconds.
5. Replace feed tube cover and let dough rise for 1 to 1 1/2 hours, or until doubled, puffed, and softened.
6. Prepare toppings (e.g., rehydrated porcini mushrooms, sautéed sweet onions with poppy seeds and toasted sesame seeds, sliced fresh Italian plum tomatoes, caponata, green bell peppers, and capers).
7. Form individual pizzas without toppings and refrigerate on nonstick cookie sheets for up to 2 hours.
8. Preheat oven to 450°F on the middle shelf. Place a baking stone if available.
9. Process dough again briefly, then turn out onto lightly floured board. Knead into 8-10 equal-sized balls (5 individual pizzas in this case).
10. Place dough balls on an oiled large baking sheet covered with a slightly damp towel and let rest for 15 minutes.
11. Press each ball into a 3-inch round, add toppings leaving a 1/4-inch border around the edge.
12. Bake for 10-12 minutes.
WARNING: Do not use aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat," and avoid words like "destroy," "burn," "splutter," or "execute."
